Welcome to

1st Gymnasio of Edessa · Erasmus+ Project Presentation
📍 Pella · Central Macedonia · Greece
Our City
Where is
Edessa?
Edessa is a beautiful city in the Pella regional unit of Central Macedonia, northern Greece. It sits at an elevation of about 320 metres on a rocky plateau overlooking a vast green plain.
  • Population of approximately 18,000 people
  • Capital city of the Pella regional unit
  • Located 90 km west of Thessaloniki
  • Known as the "City of Waters" for its famous waterfalls
  • Rich in Byzantine & ancient Macedonian history
Explore Edessa
A City of Beauty & History
🏙️ Town Square · Central Fountain
🌳 Historic Plane Tree · City Centre
🌉 Ottoman Stone Bridge
💧 City Waterfall · Waterfall Park
Natural Wonder
The Famous
Waterfalls
Edessa's waterfalls are the most iconic landmark of the city — and one of the most spectacular natural attractions in all of Greece.
  • The main waterfall drops 70 metres — one of the tallest in Greece
  • Fed by the Edessaios River flowing from the mountains
  • A waterfall park with walking paths and caves
  • You can walk behind the waterfall through a cave tunnel
  • Attracts over 200,000 visitors every year
History & Heritage
Ancient
Macedonian Roots
Edessa has a history stretching back over 2,500 years. Some historians believe it may have been the original capital of the ancient Macedonian kingdom — the homeland of Alexander the Great.
  • Possible site of Aigai — ancient Macedonian capital
  • Roman ruins dating back to 2nd century AD
  • Byzantine churches from the 14th century
  • Ottoman-era stone bridges and traditional architecture
  • Historic watermills still preserved in the city
Four Seasons City
Winter in
Edessa
In winter, the surrounding mountains transform into a snowy paradise. The Voras ski resort (Kaimaktsalan) is just 40 km away — one of the best ski destinations in northern Greece.
  • Kaimaktsalan ski resort at 2,524m altitude
  • Over 20 ski runs for all levels
  • Snow season from December to April
  • Stunning frozen chapel at the mountain summit
Our School
1st Gymnasio
of Edessa
One of the main middle schools of Edessa, bringing together students from across the city and surrounding villages in a vibrant, inclusive learning community.
~200
Students
3
Year Groups
30+
Teachers
Location of our School
Our school is located in the heart of Edessa, surrounded by nature and close to the city centre. With its large courtyard and bright classrooms, it is a welcoming place for all students.
  • Large outdoor basketball & sports court
  • Two-storey building with modern facilities
  • Green surroundings in the centre of Edessa
  • Home to over 200 students every year
Inside Our School
Where We Learn
📋 Teachers' Office
The staff room where teachers prepare lessons, grade work and are always available to help students.
🏫 Standard Classroom
Our bright classrooms fit up to 25 students. Each has a blackboard, projector and individual desks for focused learning.
Our Facilities
Labs & Resources
💻 Computer Lab
Fully equipped with PCs and projector for IT classes and digital projects.
🔬 Science Lab
Physics & Chemistry lab with real equipment — DNA models, periodic table and experiments.
📚 School Library
Hundreds of books available to all students — from textbooks to literature and reference works.
🎨 Greek Letters Art
Students created these colourful Greek letter decorations outside the Physics & Chemistry lab.
🌈 Hallway Murals
Colourful murals painted by our students decorate the school staircases and hallways.

Erasmus+
Why We Join
Erasmus+
Erasmus+ gives our students the incredible opportunity to connect with young people across Europe, share our culture, and learn about theirs.
